page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 384 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 92851.92 and a Passmark CPU score of 75961.13. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4974.71 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 328.42 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic operations perform at a top-tier level, as demonstrated by a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 87289.24. Database operations and Redis SET operations also show strong results, processing 89.82 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation represent relative performance tradeoffs.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6idn.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining substantial compute power, high-speed DDR4 memory, and a massive 5.7 TB local NVMe SSD storage array within a single general-purpose profile. This configuration reduces the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput tasks. The server is highly suited for data-intensive workloads, high-performance web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic execution, alongside a baseline network speed of 150 Gbps. While single-core speeds and large LLM inference tasks represent relative performance tradeoffs, its high-capacity local storage and balanced resource allocation provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments. It is highly suited for database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 5700 GB local NVMe SSD, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 5.7 TB NVMe SSD, delivering high-throughput networking and database performance.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive local NVMe storage, and rapid multi-core execution.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
384 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Suited for high-throughput web serving, databases, and enterprise applications
